title: teucrium chamaedrys subsp. illyricum
intro: Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. illyricum, also known as Dalmatian hyssop, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium chamaedrys, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Eastern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, as well as its ability to aid digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Dalmatian hyssop include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. Dalmatian hyssop can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or added to food and drinks, and it is also used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties.
